options.label: A browser-based interface that allows using Kiro without installing anything locally
A JavaScript framework for creating web applications with artificial intelligence
A browser extension that adds autocomplete to any online editor
A static site hosting service with automatic deploy

questions.explanation: Kiro Web is the browser-based interface of Kiro, allowing developers to use all the agent's capabilities directly in the browser without local installation.

questions.text: Kiro Web's autonomous mode allows the agent to execute tasks independently without needing approval at each step.

options.label: True
False

questions.explanation: Kiro Web's autonomous mode allows the agent to work independently on tasks, executing multiple steps without needing human approval at each stage, increasing productivity for repetitive tasks.

questions.text: What is the function of the sandbox environment in Kiro Web?

options.label: Provides an isolated cloud environment where the agent executes code and tests securely
Creates a local copy of the repository for viewing only
Blocks all internet access to protect sensitive data
Simulates production environments for load testing

questions.explanation: The sandbox environment in Kiro Web provides an isolated space in the cloud where the agent can execute code, run tests, and perform build operations securely without affecting the developer's local environment.

questions.text: A team wants to use Kiro Web to automate PR creation from issues. Which GitHub/GitLab integration best serves this scenario?

options.label: Connect the repository to Kiro Web and use tasks so the agent creates branches, implements, and opens PRs automatically
Configure a separate third-party bot that monitors issues and calls Kiro via public API
Manually export issues to a CSV file and import into Kiro Web
Use generic webhooks that send email notifications for each created issue

questions.explanation: Kiro Web has native integration with GitHub and GitLab, allowing repositories to be connected directly. Through tasks, the agent can read issues, create branches, implement solutions, and open PRs automatically.

questions.text: How do you create a task in Kiro Web?

options.label: By describing the goal in natural language in the interface, letting the agent plan and execute the steps
By writing YAML code with the exact sequence of commands to execute
By dragging visual blocks in a no-code flow editor
By selecting pre-defined templates without the possibility of customization

questions.explanation: In Kiro Web, tasks are created by describing the goal in natural language. The agent interprets the description, creates an execution plan, and implements the necessary steps autonomously in the sandbox environment.

questions.text: Specs can be created and managed directly in the Kiro Web interface without needing to manually edit files in the repository.

options.label: True
False

questions.explanation: Kiro Web offers an interface for creating and managing Specs directly in the browser. Specs are saved to the repository automatically, but the user can edit them visually without needing to manipulate files manually.

questions.text: Arrange the internet access levels of the Kiro Web sandbox from most restrictive to most permissive:

options.label: No access - sandbox completely isolated from the internet
Repository only - access only to the connected repository
Common dependencies - access to allowed package registries (npm, PyPI, Maven)
Open internet - full unrestricted internet access

questions.explanation: The Kiro Web sandbox offers progressive internet access levels: fully isolated, repository-only access (integrations), access to common package registries (dependencies), and full open internet. Authentication is managed via Identity Center.
